response:
TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF EACH CIRCLE INCREASES BY 10% FROM THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR². THE FIRST CIRCLE HAS RADIUS R, THE NEXT HAS RADIUS 1.1R, THEN 1.21R, AND SO ON. THE TOTAL AREA IS πR²(1 + (1.1)² + (1.21)² + ... + (1.4641)²). THE SUM OF THESE SQUARES IS A GEOMETRIC SERIES WITH COMMON RATIO 1.21. THE SUM OF THE SERIES IS (1 - (1.21)^7) / (1 - 1.21). THE TOTAL AREA IS πR² * (1 - (1.21)^7) / (1 - 1.21). THE ANSWER IS EXPRESSED IN TERMS OF R AND π.

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πR(1.1)^4. THE LENGTH OF THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS USED TO PLACE CHARACTERS WITH A 0.5 CM GAP. EACH CHARACTER AND GAP TAKES UP 0.5 CM. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE INTEGER PART OF THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY 0.5. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS 2πR(1.1)^4. DIVIDING BY 0.5 GIVES 4πR(1.1)^4. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE INTEGER PART OF THIS VALUE. THE ANSWER IS AN INTEGER. THE TOTAL NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS DETERMINED BY THIS CALCULATION. THE TEXT IS EVENLY SPACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE. THE DESIGNER USES TRADITIONAL TYPOGRAPHY TO ADD A TOUCH OF CLASS. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS USED AS A MEASURE FOR SPACING. THE CHARACTER COUNT IS DETERMINED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP SIZE. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS FOUND BY TAKING THE INTEGER PART. THE TEXT IS PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE. THE DESIGNER ENSURES THE TEXT IS EVENLY SPACED. THE
